weather.summary: Saturday: 9°C, scattered clouds. Sunday: 10°C, broken clouds

metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in Hackney! There are lots of exciting things happening both indoors and outdoors to keep your little one entertained. From exploring museums to enjoying outdoor adventures, there's something for everyone this weekend.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a relaxed environment for toddlers to engage with books and stories, fostering a love for reading.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Downs
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A wooden playground with a pond theme, designed for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a safe and stimulating environment for toddlers to play and explore nature-themed structures.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Stratford
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the park.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore the history of homes and domestic life through a range of exhibits and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a glimpse into different eras of home life, sparking curiosity and imagination in young children.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Hoxton
transport.tip: Check for family-friendly events on their website.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A city farm with a variety of animals, gardens, and community events.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides opportunities for children to interact with animals and learn about farming in an urban setting.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Brick Lane
transport.tip: Check their website for special Easter activities.

activities.title: DLR Driverless Trains
activities.price: Cost of travel (Children under 11 travel free)
activities.description: Ride the driverless DLR trains and sit at the front for a unique view.
activities.whyItWorks: The novelty of sitting where the driver would be is exciting for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Various routes connect to the DLR
transport.tip: Avoid peak travel times for a more comfortable ride.

activities.title: Shoreditch Street Art
activities.description: Explore the colorful street art in Shoreditch and Brick Lane.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides visual stimulation and encourages creativity.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Shoreditch High Street
transport.tip: Look for art at child-height to engage them.

activities.title: Sound Explorers at Young V&A
activities.description: Informal sessions for babies and toddlers to listen, play and make some noise.
activities.whyItWorks: Encourages sensory exploration and musical development in a relaxed setting.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Arrive early as it's first-come, first-served.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends at Young V&A
activities.description: Explore how the iconic characters are brought to life. Ticket gives unlimited visits.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and familiar characters will engage young children's imaginations.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Check for special family events on their website.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the natural world with dinosaur skeletons, fascinating exhibits, and interactive displays.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging and educational for all ages, with plenty to capture the imagination of a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Tube (Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington)
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.price: Free (IMAX and special exhibitions may require tickets)
activities.description: Interactive exhibits exploring science, technology, and medicine.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on activities and engaging displays make learning fun for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube (Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington)
transport.tip: Visit the Pattern Pod for a sensory experience for under 8s.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Indoor garden with panoramic city views.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a unique perspective of London and a chance to explore a lush indoor garden.

transport.fromHackney: Tube (various routes to Monument)
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as they are released a few weeks ahead.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world cultures and history through a vast collection of artifacts.
activities.whyItWorks: The Egyptian galleries with mummies and hieroglyphs are particularly captivating for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube (Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Oxford Circus, then Central Line to Tottenham Court Road)
transport.tip: Pick up an activity backpack or Museum Explorer Trail from the Family Desk.

activities.title: Tate Modern
activities.description: Explore modern and contemporary art with interactive exhibits and family activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a kids' room with interactive screens and a chance to experience art in a fun and engaging way.

transport.fromHackney: Tube (Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then walk or bus to Bankside)
transport.tip: Check for family workshops and activities on their website.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A large playground with a Peter Pan-themed pirate ship and sensory trails.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a fantastic outdoor space for children to play and explore, with a variety of activities to suit different ages.

transport.fromHackney: Tube (Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Oxford Circus, then Central Line to Queensway or Lancaster Gate)
transport.tip: Can get busy, especially on weekends.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: A children's playground and park with a city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a safe and enclosed space for children to play, with a variety of activities including a playground, sandpits, and a small city farm.

transport.fromHackney: Tube (Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Euston, then walk)
transport.tip: Adults are only allowed entry if accompanying a child.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore natural history, anthropology, and musical instruments, with gardens and a butterfly house.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a diverse range of exhibits and activities, including gardens, a butterfly house, and a hands-on Nature Base.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill
transport.tip: Check for family-friendly events on their website.

activities.title: The Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a fun treasure hunt and a sweet reward.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance online.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Themed giant Easter egg trail with clues and challenges, plus hands-on activities and live entertainment.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of indoor and outdoor play areas, animal enclosures, and interactive activities.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Hounslow Heath
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance online.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for giant Easter eggs.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a zoo visit with a fun Easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance online for a discount.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze at Little Angel Studios
activities.description: Puppet show about a little old lady who finds her house is too small.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppetry is engaging for young children, and the show is based on a popular story.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Highbury & Islington
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend shows.

activities.title: The Everywhere Bear at Little Angel Theatre
activities.description: A bear goes on his own big adventure when he falls unnoticed from a backpack
activities.whyItWorks: Aimed at young children, uses puppetry.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Highbury & Islington
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend shows.

activities.title: I Want My Hat Back Trilogy at Little Angel Studios
activities.description: Jon Klassen's beloved books, I Want My Hat Back, This is Not My Hat and We Found A Hat, have found a new life of their own LIVE on stage.
activities.whyItWorks: Aimed at young children, uses puppetry.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Highbury & Islington
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend shows.

activities.title: Playing Shakespeare with Deutsche Bank: Romeo and Juliet at Shakespeare's Globe
activities.description: Critically acclaimed 90-min version created especially for young people. (Plus Romeo & Juliet Family Workshop (9-11) 28 Mar-11 Apr, £10.)
activities.whyItWorks: Excellent for introducing kids to Shakespeare.

transport.fromHackney: Tube
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Fairy Tales at the British Library
activities.description: A new interactive exhibition which explores enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, through books, costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations from some of the UKs most beloved writers.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a magical experience for families.

transport.fromHackney: Tube
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.
